revision.text:
[[ja:伊勢国造]] '''Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (伊勢国造, Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko) was a ''{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}'' (regional chieftain) who governed the area later known as {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} in ancient Japan. == Overview == === Name === Recorded as '''Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}} section of the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}''. === Ancestry === * According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} was appointed as the first ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' by Emperor Jimmu. * According to surviving excerpts from the lost ''Ise no Kuni Fudoki'', Emperor Jimmu dispatched '''{{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106107046}}''' to pacify {{ill|Isetsuhiko|en|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127|lt=Isetsuhiko|1=Isetsuhiko}}. His descendants, through {{ill|Hikokunimigaki-tate-Yotsuka-no-Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki-tate-Yotsuka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrined at {{ill|Watarai Kuni Mikami Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kuni Mikami Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486118}}), became the Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The ruling clan was the '''{{ill|Ise Clan|ja|伊勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Clan|1=Ise clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16141593}}''' (Ise-uji), holding the hereditary title (Kabane) of ''Atai'' (直). Their descendants became known as '''Nakatomi Ise''', later gaining the titles of ''Muraji'' (連) in 747, ''Ason'' (朝臣) in 764, and finally adopting the surname '''Ise Ason''' in 766. Another related family was the {{ill|Isobe Clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588086}}, later known as {{ill|Watarai Clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Clan|1=Watarai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486123}}, priests of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}'s Outer Shrine. == Base of Operations == Their administrative center was in '''{{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|qid=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District|1=Suzuka District, Mie}}''' of Ise Province, specifically around {{ill|Kokufu Village (Mie Prefecture)|ja|国府村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Village (Mie Prefecture)|1=koumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420870}}. === Territory === The territory of Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko likely included {{ill|Kawawa District|ja|河曲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawawa District|1=Kawawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11553861}}, {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|qid=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District|1=Suzuka District, Mie}}, and [[:en:Mie District, Mie|Mie District]], excluding regions controlled by other local powers like {{ill|Iga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊賀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380648}}, {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, and various smaller clans in southern Ise Province. == Tutelary Deities == The primary shrine worshipped by the clan is unknown, though {{ill|Kuwana Sōja|ja|桑名宗社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwana Sōja|1=Kuwana Sōsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11537501}} (in Kuwana City, Mie Prefecture) is a prominent shrine dedicated to Ame-no-Hiwake-no-Mikoto. === Related Shrine === * {{ill|Nakato Shrine|ja|奈加等神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakato Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 都波岐神社・奈加等神社|1=Tsubaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11643733}} – Located in Suzuka City; founded by Takaotsuka-no-Mikoto during Emperor Yūryaku's reign. == Genealogy == The clan's lineage according to the ''Toyōke Daijingū Negi Honin Shidai'' in ''Gunsho Ruijū'' is: == 系図　== 『[[群書類従]]』に所収されている「豊受太神宮禰宜補任次第」によれば、系図は以下の通りである[https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/DAS/meta/listPhoto?LANG=default&BID=F1000000000000037305&ID=M1000000000000065387&NO=&TYPE=JPEG&DL_TYPE=pdf 豊受太神宮禰宜補任次第 No.133]『[[群書類従]]』「豊受太神宮禰宜補任次第」。
;実線は実子、点線は養子。 {{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[:en:カミムスビ|神魂命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[櫛間乳魂命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[天底立命|天曽己多智命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''天嗣桙命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''天鈴桙命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''天御雲命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[天牟良雲命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[天波与命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''[[天日別命]]'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''彦国見賀岐建与束命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''彦多都久禰命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''彦楯津命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''彦久良為命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0| |)|-|-|.}} {{familytree |border=0|01|02|01='''[[乙若子命]]'''|02=[[大若子命]]}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''爾佐布命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''彦和志理命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''阿波良波命'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''乙乃古命'''}} {{familytree/end}}
== Notable Figures == * '''Ame-no-Hiwake-no-Mikoto''' – First Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko appointed during Emperor Jimmu’s reign. * '''Takaotsuka-no-Mikoto''' – Credited with founding Nakato Shrine during Emperor Yūryaku's reign. == Descendants == * {{ill|Ise no Tsuguko|ja|伊勢継子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Tsuguko|1=Ise no Tsugiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11379118}} – Wife of Emperor Heizei; daughter of Nakatomi no Ise no Oi. Mother of Prince Takaoka and Prince Kose. * {{ill|Watarai Ieyuki|ja|度会家行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ieyuki|1=Watarai Ieyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486121}} – Prominent Shinto priest; established Ise Shinto during Kamakura to Nanboku-chō period. == References == {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * {{ill|Ise Clan|ja|伊勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Clan|1=Ise clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16141593}} * {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi Clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shinto Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|伊勢国造|version=103079309|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伊勢国造]] {{wikidata link|Q11378974}} [[Category:History of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Clan]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
